items.trigger: Mabel asked for a scoping outline after the intro call
items.valueToRecipient: A one-page scoping outline for the audit she asked about
evidence: eng-s12: replied to the diagnostic newsletter asking to talk scoping
items.message:
Hi Mabel,

Good to talk last week. You asked for a scoping outline for the commercial systems piece, so here it is, one page as promised.

The short version: two weeks of looking at how deals actually move through Harbourline, then a prioritised plan you could hand to the team. If the shape looks right, happy to walk through it on a short call.

Stuart

reply.text: This looks right. Can we do Thursday?

items.valueToRecipient: A guest episode on turning commercial chaos into systems, with practical material his audience can use
items.message:
Hi Dominic,

September works. The angle I'd bring: why commercial systems break in founder-led businesses, told through specifics rather than frameworks. Three stories, each with the fix.

If that fits the show, Aisyah mentioned the 10th or the 17th.

Stuart

items.trigger: Rory mentioned flat sponsorship renewals three years running
items.valueToRecipient: The sponsor outcome map exercise, which he can run internally even if we never work together
items.message:
Hi Rory,

Been thinking about the flat-renewals point since our coffee. One exercise that might be useful regardless of anything else: map what each sponsor could claim internally after the event. If the answer is thin, the renewal conversation was lost before it started.

Happy to talk through how I'd run that if useful.

Stuart

reply.text: Interesting. Send me the shape of it.

items.trigger: Annelies asked about sponsor measurement plans under the renewals post
items.valueToRecipient: A concrete answer to the measurement question she raised publicly
items.message:
Hi Annelies,

Your question under my sponsorship post deserved a longer answer than a comment. The measurement plan I use has three layers: what the sponsor claims internally, what the audience did, and what renewed. Happy to share the one-pager.

No agenda beyond that; the question was a good one.

Stuart

items.trigger: Third meaningful engagement in a fortnight, latest on renewals and churn
items.valueToRecipient: A direct exchange on the discount-authority question he keeps raising
evidence: eng-s03, eng-s04, eng-s05: three comments in two weeks
items.message:
Hi Tom,

You've asked two of the sharpest questions under my pricing posts recently, and the churn point today made me want to take it out of the comments.

How are you handling discount authority at Parallax today? Asking because the below-20-staff version is genuinely different and I'm still testing my own answer.

Stuart

items.trigger: Northlight programmes autumn summits around now (fictional)
items.valueToRecipient: A talk where the audience self-diagnoses their commercial systems, which fits his practitioner-track brief
evidence: int-s17: Pieter conversation referenced the autumn programme
items.message:
Hi Oliver,

Pieter mentioned you're shaping the autumn programme. I have a talk that's been landing well in founder rooms: the commercial systems audit as a live exercise, where the audience diagnoses their own operation while listening.

If the practitioner track has space, happy to send the abstract.

Stuart

items.valueToRecipient: A concrete pilot shape both sides can take to one client each
evidence: int-s18: second call on pilot shape
items.message:
Hi Josh,

Following our call: the pilot shape that makes sense to me is one client each, same diagnostic format, then we sit down and see what the data layer needs to feed it.

If that still works for you, I'll draft the one-pager this week.

Stuart

items.trigger: His fund published a SEA SaaS GTM note adjacent to my founder-dinner observation
items.valueToRecipient: A ground-level data point for a thesis his fund is publicly developing
evidence: int-s09: panel conversation on SEA SaaS GTM
items.message:
Hi Wei Lun,

Your fund's GTM note crossed my feed and it matched something I keep hearing at founder dinners here: nobody asks about models, everyone asks what stopped being painful. Thought the ground-level version might be useful to the thesis.

No ask; the note was good.

Stuart
